reviews.review_text:
Devs have been making changes the community never asked for or wanted. Changing iconic maps without even an option to go back to the original is a weird L move to make. Changing the game to be an objectively worse version of itself is really dumb. 
I love playing with my friends, but the devs keep making L changes like they have a gun to the back of their heads. Lock in devs. Just make the damn game, you got Repo over here showing you how it's done but you won't take the hint. (the hint is that there are other co-op games that have more caring, consistent and hard working devs with less team numbers who arguably deserve even more of a community at this point over this. You've made hundreds of millions of dollars, stop milking the early access and make better decisions)
My thoughts on the game are that it's good and I love the ghost interactions, but the devs are losing my trust with bad decisions that seem to lack consideration to their community. This game is not in a good state right now, but can be so much better.

It's not a lake, it's an ocean
It's not a loop, it's a spiral
It's not a Demon, it's a Mimic
Congrats on turning the game into a cheap knockoff of itself.

c'mon, y'all went from having a huge reputation to everyone being disappointed until to the point where the empathy is not worth it. There should be no excuse for this horrible "update". The game is LITERALLY unplayable now and it's so embarrassing, the gameplay is js raw I genuinely don’t understand how it’s this bad..

Phas, it breaks my heart to leave a negative review. I've played your game for over 1770 hours. I've watched you push repetitive event content and ignore bugs for years. I've heard your promises of roadmap updates heard your promises of bug fixes, seen your lack of transparency (one holiday event was never even announced on steam till it was halfway over) and heard the stories of toxicity. When the player customization update got pushed and I saw just how big of a step backward it was, I told myself that surely this couldn't have been what we had been waiting for three years for you to deliver. The collab with Alan Wake? is a insult to Alan Wake. I hope that you take some time to seriously look at your game and your community and see how broken it is, how unhappy we are. But, if I'm being honest, I just don't think you will. The empty promises have eroded my trust to the point where I have none left. Hopefully you wake up before more people like me have moved on. I wanted SO much for you to succeed. I still do. Please be better. Do better. Fix the bugs. Focus on finishing your game before you collaborate with other people. Be more transparent. actually LISTEN to the feedback and community full of people who despite everything still want you to succeed.
Released a very bad update and told players to "just play more" and to get used to it.

It’s hard for me to look at Phasmophobia without thinking about how I started. A girl I’m no longer close with was the one who actually got me into this; I remember how confusing and even a bit intimidating the game felt at first. But thanks to her, I learned the ropes and eventually got over that initial fear. It turned into genuine, "locked-in" fun that kept me playing for hours, right up until we stopped talking.

Fast forward to a week ago: my friend group finally picked up the game after I’d been begging them for months. I reinstalled it, hyped to get back, and was immediately met with a mess. The menus are laggy and clunky, and the lobby looks awful. It’s slow and feels completely "off." They’ve tried to make the shop menu look "technologically improved," but they failed. It looks terrible. The old menu was simple and straightforward; you knew exactly what you were buying. There was no reason to overcomplicate a design that was already perfect just for the sake of a "modern" look.

The actual gameplay isn't much better. Once you finally manage to load in, the experience is chaotic. My crew instantly snatched up all the essential starter items, leaving me wandering around clueless among weird new models and unnecessary item changes. The UI on the equipment is a huge step back - the EMF reader is tiny and hard to read, and the thermometer is just as bad. They’ve added a bunch of new items that don't actually seem to serve a purpose for the ghost hunting itself. While I’ll admit a couple of the models look decent, most of it just feels like change for the sake of change.

You’d think Phasmophobia would have kept the simplicity and charm that made it fun in the first place, but it hasn’t. It’s trying too hard to be something new and, in the process, has taken ten steps backward.

I even saw where the lead designer basically said, "I didn't make the new models, I just approved them." That’s a weak excuse. If you approved them, you’re responsible for the state of the game. You're admitting you watched the game’s aesthetic get ruined without stepping in to say it looked wrong.

Then there’s the new EULA. They basically state that they have the full right to shut the game down, remove it from your library, and collect your personal information. You have to be kidding me. You ruin the game with creepy character models, unreadable gadgets, and new bugs, and on top of that, you declare the right to take the game away and harvest data? It honestly feels like a "Plan B" for Kinetic Games in case the player base drops off because they don't like the direction the game is heading.

Honestly? It’s not even worth it on sale right now, let alone at full price. The "improvements" have stripped away the magic the game used to have.

Amidst the huge animation update controversy, I decided to finally go back to Phasmo after spending a few hundred hours on console and putting the game down. We all know the new update is well, ♥♥♥♥, but I wanted to keep an open mind and play a few matches to see what the current state of this game is, and not just hop on a trend to just relay what someone says online.

[h3] Yeah, it's bad. [/h3]
At it's core, it's still Phasmo, and for the most part it plays the same. But god, this new update looks and feels BEYOND terrible. The new characters look mid at best. A lot of the female ones look especially terrible. The new cosmetics are okay? But are insanely overpriced. Like I would understand having a few really cool exotic-looking pants go for $10k, but only ONE average pants cosmetic costing $10k? WTF!
I only played around 4 matches, and I encountered probably more than 10+ significant bugs, some of which actually affected my experience beyond visual glitches. 
My legs would wrap around each other like spaghetti when turning side-to-side, many of the animations are clunky and take way too long making picking up items during a hunt nearly impossible, the hands for my character and the way they try to hold objects looks comparable to how a 5 year old would draw hands, the character and the stuff they're holding jitters when moving or crouching and gave me a headache just looking at it, and even the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ shoes on my character's feet are swapped! They couldn't even put the right shoes on my feet!!!

[h3] Incompetency. [/h3] 
It's obvious this update was rushed, but the devs have allegedly been working on this for more than 2 years! TF had they been doing all this time? The devs had years, and [b] millions [/b] of dollars, and yet it looks like they took no more than a week to actually develop this update.
I feel like this update should mark a changing point for how the devs treat this game, and highlights a bunch of long-standing issues that have plagued this game. For years, it's suffered from poor quality assurance, un-optimization (don't get me started on the console ports), bugs that remain unfixed, and overall a poor return in quality updates for how much damn money Kinetic Games have made from this game! FROM US! 
Kinetic Games are [b] no longer [/b] an Indie studio, they have millions of dollars to use to make these updates awesome, and yet we're still facing bugs and the same ghost models that were ripped straight from a pre-made model and animation pack made by someone else years ago.
Just look at any other ghost-hunting games, the quality is much better and they had less money to use! And yet, here is Phasmo taking months to years to release a half-baked update and expecting to get away with it. 

I will consider posting a more broad and game-centered review once this update is fixed, because as it stands this one update really shows how little Kinetic Games are willing to do with everything at their disposal. They can have a genuine review when THEY ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ EARN ONE!

Edit: Phasmo has since released some bugfixes and small tweaks, but the character models and animations still look terrible. Invest your time in another game.

This game is going nowhere. 

In the last, like, 2 years, we only got a few map remakes and a new map or something? Now, we got the character update, and it's so full of bugs and glitches, it's unbelievable. 

The game doesn't need new maps. It doesn't need character customization (not at this point, there are things that are desperately needed much more than this). It needs a gameplay loop that is actually engaging, challenging in new ways, and has high replayability. That's what Horror 2.0 would bring, which btw was first mentioned like 4 years ago, and in 2026 is still only a legend that might or might not exist...

All this after getting about 200 million dollars of profit from this game. They should be ashamed for not hiring a dev team that is actually capable of making a finished game out of phasmophobia.

And the devs ban people who mod the game or release mods. I think they are afraid of fans creating a much better experience from phasmo via mods than themselves.

This game was first released in 2020(?) and it's now in a worse state than in 2020.

I don't even know where to begin with the latest update, and this one had a massive list of fixes. The 'customisation' is worse than a PS2 game. The animations are unnatural, jarring and broken. Equipment is sometimes half off the screen. The journal isn't readable now because it's zoomed out?

And during a game it's even harder since people clipping through it flashes the text.

I've been critical of the design direction (why remake old maps people like???) but this time it's not just that, the game is not currently unplayable.

When will you start listening to the whole community instead of circle-jerking inside discord. Stop making deals for merch and start hiring game developers who know how to make games. The response as well "I didn't make it I just signed off on it" shows a level of arrogance and contempt.

I cannot believe you signed off on this update. Years and years of Dev and THIS is what you think is an acceptable release?

I don’t think there’s much I can add that hasn’t already been said.  I’ve played this game since release. I have over a thousand hours, I completed all of the achievements on steam. As far as games go, I definitely have gotten my money’s worth. Although, from the beginning, Phas felt like something more than most other games. It was a saving grace through the pandemic. I made a lot of really great memories with friends that quarantine/health precautions made it difficult to spend time with in person. I met new friends who are still in my life nearly 6 years later. All because of a silly ghost hunting game where cabinets would occasionally spawn in the walkway and if you clicked on a door in just the right spot, you could catapult yourself to the roof of prison.  It was janky, but Tanglewood on a stormy night always felt like home. 

I know that it sounds kind of parasocial, but update days used to feel like literal holidays. I couldn’t wait to wake up at 5-6 in the morning, update my game as soon as the update dropped, and explore the new maps or ghosts that were released.  At a point, there was an incredible synergy between DK, Lavender, and CJ.  You could tell that they genuinely loved their game. They’d tease new features they were working on, and they were happy to engage with Phasmophobia fans on Twitch and discord. 
Things started to go downhill right before the equipment rework.  PR nightmares, Reddit drama (but what else is new there?) I think the team realized that they had truly built something more than just a game. There was a crossroads of continuing on that same upward path, or branching out and letting ego take the wheel. Kinetic chose the latter and each update since then has run the game deeper into the ground, while the heads of kinetic sit on their thrones and watch it burn. 
Don’t report bugs on discord, that’s what kinetic partners are for. Have an opinion different from the devs or their friends? Comments deleted.  User-friendly UI? Creative, engaging ghost behavior? Nah. Updated farmhouses. Tanglewood rework.  That’s what you really want. You want characters with crinkly fingers and FOV that makes you nauseated. You don’t want new ghost models and interactions, you want a board game and a movie.  

Kinetic has *never* listened to their fanbase. The team purposely built a cliquey community who will parrot back the things that they want to hear, for a tiny bit of recognition and maybe a couple bucks every time they stream the game, rather than taking to heart the critiques of day one fans who genuinely mean well, despite not having as big of an audience for the “exclusive” and “fancy” little Kinetic Partner badge. 

I genuinely wish the best for Kinetic and the future of Phas, but if things continue on the same trajectory that they have over the last couple of years, it’s just going to be yet another example of how success and money can suck the soul out of even the most well intentioned passion projects.
I don’t know if a potential new player would be swayed one way or another by a single steam review, so I’ll leave you with some paraphrased words of CJ, the kinetic art director when faced with this new round of negative criticism: “The game is perfectly playable,. if you want to play it, you’ll eventually get used to the jank”

I find it truly magical that even a single person thought that launching this update was a good idea. How in the ever loving jesus do you not see that essentially every single new animation is buggy? How do you not notice the constants jitters? The clipping of objects? The inability to read half the info on equipment? The fact that everything breaks even more if you're not on lowest FOV? The in your face of every piece of equipment in the game? The clipping of clothing inside your character? The unreadable wrist watch? The dislocating wrists the moment you dare to look up and down? I'll answer this question for you: they didn't miss it. They just didn't give a ♥♥♥♥. They hope that as always, they will overpromise, underdeliver then have the streamers and creators come bail them out. They got popular and greedy. So sad to see from a company that had so much promise. What kind of character customisation update doesn't even add a separate female body, making all new characters be the literal exact same but with a different head? Lazy, greedy, malicious, insulting, unprofessional. Lastly, im absolutely sick and tired of this new trend where games exist in early access for a child's entire elementary school life worth of time, then proceed to use early access as a pretence for the lackluster content. Well no guys, I'm sorry. I want to be on your side but you've milked the last bit of patience i had.
Versions 0.17.0.0 and higher are just unplayable. The latest updates just ruined this game. The game became absolutely casual with millions of bugs.

How on God’s green earth has every update since release managed to make this game fundamentally worse. I don't know why y'all bothered making this a live-service game. It should've been left alone in the perfectly acceptable and enjoyable state it was when it first released.

Playing this game again after not playing for a few months, and holy hell the controls, the animations, and the sounds are all terrible. The journal animation takes 3 seconds to render? Why isn't it 1 second like it used to be? It feels way too sluggish. And my handheld thermostat was making camera picture noises whenever I tried reading the temperature. While holding the video camera, it spazzes out a lot, especially when you are switching from crouching to standing, vice versa. I thought I was going insane because I don't remember the game having so many bugs with sounds, and clipping animations when you hold items, but it seems like everyone is hating this update just as much as me. Some more thought should have been put into testing this update before releasing it to the public, and even after 1 or 2 days of it being released and receiving much negative feedback, the update should be reverted.

Edit: They've fixed a lot of the bugs, but the camera is still wonky while you hold it and crouch/stand up. Also if you're holding the T2 parabolic mic and swap to the video camera, your audio is still stuck to the parabolic mic until you switch back to the parabolic and turn it off.

TL;DR - A once great game ruined by the greed of an upcoming collab, and incompetent development leads throwing their teams under the proverbial bus. The game is currently broken, the devs know it's broken, they don't seem to care.

I'd have recommended this game highly until this point. It's a fun puzzle game that masquerades as horror, and it's good at it. I've got a few hours in for that reason

This latest update, however, is a total joke. Ghosts are broken, the animations are a disaster, the cosmetics (Well, the few options that there are...) clip through the character models, evidence items are held ridiculously far away from the camera, the torch shines a light on the journals so you can't read it...

I could go on, but I think it gets the point across. The worst bit is you don't even have to dig to find these issues, they're right there in your face as you start playing. Many are mentioned right there in the patch notes as "upcoming fixes", so the devs knew just how busted this update is yet launched it anyway ready for the Alan Wake collab to get that sweet, sweet cash.

I can't end this review without mentioning the remarks of the art director, CJ. In a now-deleted post (You can find a screenshot of the original post, as well as additional staff confirmation that the post was authentic, in the Patch Day Megathread on the PhasmophobiaGame subreddit), he threw his entire team under the bus by absolving himself of any responsibility for how bad the animations are because, in his words, he "just approved things as they came in". I'll say it again: This is the art *director*. He also said that with time players will "just get used to the jank" and deployed the whole "still in early access" defense, but those are just cherries on the top at the point.

I've tried to hold my tongue for as long as I can, I'm not a developer, so I don't know all the work that goes into making a game, but as a player I can say that the game doesn't even work the way it's supposed to. How exactly do you take an 'overwhelmingly positive' game and turn it into this, and keep it that way when more than half of your players are saying the same things? To me, it should have been reverted as soon as people were saying, well, everything that has already been said. I couldn't even get passed the training level, because the spiritbox didn't work. It could hear my voice, but the ghost just doesn't respond. How are you supposed to gain new players when nothing works? Especially since a lot of the players that have been here for years are leaving due to a lack of caring. 

It saddens me to see a game I loved in such a state. There is "jank" in a game, and then there is this. When the game stops working as it is intended, you do not say 'get used to it', you admit you did wrong and make it better. The "apology" we got came with a hidden message about the Alan Wake collab, and it was kind of just a slap in the face. It has been a little over two weeks and the fundamentals of the game are still broken to the point where it isn't enjoyable. Everything should have been put on hold until it was completely fixed, but according to the road map you're still working on new maps, and re-vamps, as well as the bug fixes. I wished nothing but the best for this game, but I don't see it changing anytime soon.

It is with a heavy heart that I write this review saying that I will be leaving Phasmo, and probably avoiding anything these developers may do in the future. I had some great times on here, but the fun is over for me. I can not recommend this game in its current state. I will be looking for another game that my friends and I can play.

Phasmophobia exhibits some of the worst qualities of modern videogame development. This ostensibly singleplayer game sports server-based account checking that has banned players for modifying their saves for more money or experience (when unlocks are behind level progression, but we'll get there,) and even for just modding the game, even if they only play with friends or solo.

Progression is locked behind level ups. Very quickly you'll find that you need to play in the hardest difficulties and perform all objectives for extra rewards at the end of the stage. That will get you 1/3rd of the way to a level up. This is just to unlock the chance to BUY items that would be fun to play with. Then you have to actually buy them, which for some reason costs thousands just to unlock the tier.

Fun, inoffensive bugs get patched out, like the ability to break doors off their hinges, solely because streamers could use it to go through doors slightly faster. Also, the game has been made progressively harder because of these streamers as well, including progression becoming snail paced. Turns out that if you play the same game for 5 hours a day, 5 days a week, you become pretty good at it; it would be a shame if you are then used as a measuring scale to make the rest of the playerbase have a miserable time in comparison.

Now we are doing 2x EXP and Money weeks? Just why? This game likes to believe it's GTA Online or some live service game while failing to realise that the playerbase that shot it up to fame left long ago when it became a slog.

I didn't even play the latest update after seeing what a disaster it is. The new player models look bad, break animations and the update has introduced numerous bugs. There are much better indie games out there you can play with friends which respect your time much more. Just play one of those instead. Maybe pour one out for the game this used to be.

P.S.: There's 4 years between Witcher 1 and Witcher 2, and 4 more years between Witcher 2 and Witcher 3. Six years later, this game is still in early access and we're still waiting for a real "horror" update.

How does a studio after 6 year in early access and gaining multi-Millions in profits release a character customization that makes the game look worse than when it released, not to mention the ridiculous amounts of money needed for some of these items. But what's the worse part is that this supposedly has been in the works for years if what the Lead Art Dev said in 2023 is true, putting Horror 2.0 lower on the to do list and focusing on Character Customizations since then....we all saw how bad these animations are, either they haven't been working on this or just never listened to anything the QA team said, possibly both unless we hear from the dev's themselves. If this is them putting in massive amount's of work for it to turn out badly then what's to say Horror 2.0 won't have the same fate? i truly don't see myself ever coming back to this game with just how bad they messed up what was supposed to be the main update to turn this in their vision of the game and not just some asset flip it started as.

The game, when it works, is fun and has been for years. What is not fun is the lack of demonstrable skill at Kinetic Games in understanding and developing their own game and giving it the respect it, and the players, deserve. 

The Customisation update that has rolled out has embarrassingly poor quality rigging and animation - animators please next time get a mirror and a video camera and try the poses out BEFORE you model them in game and learn about IK constraints to stop bone-breaking poses, critical mechanical issues that break gameplay - various items and tools do not function as they should leading to deaths and gear/money loss, and an overall rushed, untested, unpolished and frustrating experience. 

For a game this far into it's dev cycle and built off the back of pre-made and un-updated assets, and with a significant amount of income, it's shameful to push out something as poor quality as this. If the game mechanics don't work you cannot play the game.

Do better Kinetic Games.

When I bought Phasmophobia, it was during one of the early Alpha builds. It was a fantastic little arcade experience! I was genuinely excited to see more. However, it seems that since then, the devs have been insisting on building up an incredibly grindy progression system. 

No longer can I load the game up alone, buy a couple of the items I want and go for a few runs. Now I have to grind myself from low levels all the way up to even access some of the basic equipment, let alone their levelled equivalents. 

There's another development, however, that I've become exceedingly displeased about. Phasmophobia is still listed as an "Early Access" game. However, the developers have seen fit to be host to a crossover update with Alan Wake 2. I won't be discussing any content of the update, I am just very disappointed to see that development is being directed away from the game itself for this crossover. If the game is indeed already in a good enough place that development time can be taken away from the primary game, why on earth is it still listed as Early Access?

I don't have any words. 

The current state of the game is genuinely close to unplayable after the recent update.
I went from loving the game, to disliking it and its developers practically overnight.

Kinetic needs to bring on some new talent with the millions they've banked, because this is going completely wrong.

670 hours later and I genuinely miss when this game was janky in a charming way instead of janky in a corporate way.

The recent character update honestly broke something for me. After all this time this is what we waited for? Lifeless models. Stiff animations. Awkward proportions. Hands and items taking up half the screen. Equipment interactions feel delayed and clunky now. Even with immersive animations turned off the game somehow still feels worse to play. I genuinely cannot believe this update was pushed live in this state after years of development. The old system was not perfect but at least it stayed out of the way. Now I feel like I am fighting the UI more than the ghost. The journal sits so far away that I practically need to headbutt my monitor just to read evidence clearly. Item swapping feels delayed. The flashlight system feels awkward. Watching characters hold equipment now looks like they are trying to carry groceries during an earthquake.

And somehow all of this came before fixing problems people have talked about for years. Bugs. Performance. Optimization. Ghost behavior. The game keeps getting visually reworked while basic gameplay jank somehow survives every patch like an immortal ghost type nobody can identify. This game used to feel tense and immersive because it was simple. You walked into a house with your friends and tried not to die while the game scared the hell out of you through atmosphere and unpredictability. Now half the gameplay on bigger maps feels like Paranormal Equipment Hauling Simulator 2026. At this point just let me bring a shopping cart into Sunny Meadows so I can transport my fifteen flashlights and emotional baggage in one trip. And somehow this is still called Early Access. That label made sense years ago when this felt like a small passion project held together with duct tape and caffeine. It makes a lot less sense after millions of copies sold and years of success while updates somehow release feeling less polished than builds from years ago.

That is the part that really gets me. Phasmophobia had lightning in a bottle. The early versions were messy but they had personality. The game felt like it was made by people having fun making a horror game. Now it feels like every update sands away more of that identity in exchange for this weird corporate cleanliness that still somehow releases broken. The saddest part is I used to defend this game constantly. Back before Version 1.0. Back before every update started feeling like one step forward and two hunts backward. Back when the game understood that being fun mattered more than pretending to be some sanitized live service platform. Now it feels like the devs are more focused on image and control than actually addressing years of bugs and jank people have complained about forever. The community keeps pointing out the same problems every update and every update somehow introduces three more.

I do not hate this game. I think that is why my review exists.

I hate watching something I genuinely loved slowly lose the personality that made people care about it in the first place. :(
25 MILLION copies sold and 6 years in early access and it's still basically the same game. How do they do it

"indie game with a small team" but it's a game with half a billion dollars in sales since its release (6yo game btw). the latest update is a joke, and their partnered streamers won't even admit it. maybe hire more animators and actually care about players' feedbacks?
[b] [strike] Mentally deficient [/strike] devs [/b] do nothing but merch deals and game-ruining updates

I've been playing this game with a lot of love, but the last update broke te game for me. The character creation feels cheap, unfinished and clunky. The POV is all weird now with how you're holding your equipment and the game feels more laggy and buggy than before.

Please, undo the last update and work a little more into perfecting what you want to add. You cannot tell me that this is what you're proud enough of to release. For this reason, I have to press "No" on the recommendation.

How in God's name does adding new character animations and models screw up the function of almost EVERYTHING in the game. I've seen Thayes that don't slow down, Onis acting like Poltergests, and ghosts of all kinds using the Deogen ability to know where you are at all times. These are functions (and other items that have broken such as the spirit box and firelight) that should be in NO WAY affected by the customisation update. The fact that what should be a simple cosmetic update has ruined the game and made it near unplayable indicates an insanely chaotic programming process with muddled code and next to no QA testing.

I've played this game for several years now and followed their journey to become a complete game. In the beginning, I was hopeful and excited for everything they had in store and with every update, it got progressively better. After all, with almost 300 hours on this game, it shows I've enjoyed it, and had some really good times hunting with friends.

However, the last two years have been anything but great for this game. To still be in early access, despite it not being that heavy of a game, feels like a joke. Their roadmap for 2026, still has major updates planned (e.g. horror 2.0) that has been promised since several years back. Their promises of release dates for major events, always pushed back due to different reasons. Their latest character update that has been in the works for years, somehow managed to take the game several steps back as it is packed with bugs, and quite honestly, looks absolutely terrible. Their holiday events are just copy paste of previous years. All these faults, and they choose to put their time and resources on things like unnecessary collabs, a board game and a movie instead. I'm not saying these are bad things, but I'd like to see a FINISHED game, before they pour their time and effort into other related things.

I want to love this game. I did when I first started playing it and I have been so excited for everything it had potential to be. But I feel like the people behind this game are doing anything but finishing the game. I have seen games with just a fraction of this studio's resources produce games of better quality, significantly faster. I have lost my trust and slowly losing my support for this game as it, somehow, manages to get worse over time. I don't recommend anyone to play this until it's out of early access. Which clearly, won't be anytime soon.

In 2022, they said theyd make cosmetics, and so for 4 years, in game there was a cosmetics tab that said "Coming soon"

Now, cosmetics are out. And after 4 years of waiting, we got 2 pairs of pants, each 10k in game to unlock, 0 hair style customization, 5 color options for hair, 3 base shirts (plus some color variations), 4 jacket options (no option to not wear a jacket), a handful of glasses, peircings, and 8 rings.

I used to love playing this game with my friends, but the new cosmetics update + all the animation stuff, it doesnt feel like the game I loved. Iv seen people refer to it as its like a cheap phasmo knock off, and theyre spot on with that

I have played this game far too much over the past 6 years, gotten much laughs, scares and memories from it. After a few unsavory updates, and the sheer laziness when it came to the events, forcing people to get rewards based on a community progress rather than personal, then the mess of a customization update last week, I can safely say to not buy this game or support these devs going forward. 6 years in EA, and still no end in sight.

A tone-deaf message from their Art Director surely wasn't a great idea, whether it was deleted or not. Still shows their views and feelings.

This update is a huge step backward. The game is now unoptimized and choppy. When strafing left to right, there's significant frame drops. The evidence book is now 1/2 the size of the screen and almost illegible. (and I'm playing on a large display sitting relatively close). 

These new annoying hand movements when operating the equipment are a poor design choice. It's almost like the game wasn't QA'd at all. Did any of the developers walk around taking temperatures? Your characters hand is constantly moving back and forth, again making the text illegible. Same with the cameras; when you're walking with the cameras they're constantly shaking back and forth. Phasmophobia: The scariest part is that this update was approved

Phasmophobia used to be a ghost hunting game. Now it feels like a haunted tech demo made by a sleep-deprived raccoon with Unity open on one monitor and a glue stick in its mouth.

This game is the year’s hottest, latest, freshest piece of premium-grade garbage. Not regular garbage either. This is artisanal garbage. Locally sourced. Handcrafted. Aged in early access for nearly six years until it became sentient and started attacking the player base.

Every update somehow feels like the developers found the last update, looked at it, said “this is too playable,” and then threw a wrench into the nearest fan. The game has sold tens of millions of copies, public estimates put the revenue somewhere in the “how are we still having these problems?” range, and yet the latest update feels like it was quality tested by someone who launched the game, saw the main menu, whispered “ship it,” and immediately went to lunch.

The new player models are awful. I do not mean “a little rough.” I mean they look like mannequins that escaped from a discount clothing store during a gas leak. The old models were janky, sure, but at least they had charm. The new ones look like somebody tried to modernize the game by removing the soul and replacing it with an awkward biology class skeleton wearing Temu jeans.

The character physics are somehow worse than before. Watching players move around now feels less like paranormal investigation and more like watching four possessed CPR dummies try to survive a warehouse fire. Arms flail. Bodies bend weirdly. Equipment clips, shakes, stutters, blocks the screen, and generally behaves like it is also trying to leave the game.

The ghosts are still basically zombies with a bad attitude. For a game called Phasmophobia, you would think “ghosts” would be a core visual priority. Instead, half the time it looks like I am being hunted by an angry sleepwalker from a PS2 horror game who got lost on the way to a Spirit Halloween.

The equipment update is a disaster. Cameras are nearly unusable. The shaking is unbearable. Trying to walk and record footage feels like your character is filming paranormal evidence during an earthquake while riding a shopping cart downhill. The field of view feels like it was designed by a toddler holding binoculars backwards. Actually, that is insulting to toddlers. A toddler would at least accidentally make something playable.

The camera view is so bad that I am no longer scared of the ghost. I am scared of eye strain. The game is laggy, shaky, visually uncomfortable, and physically annoying to play. Nothing says “immersive horror” like turning the game off because your eyeballs are filing a workers’ compensation claim.

This latest update is the one that finally broke me. It does not feel polished. It does not feel tested. It does not feel like a studio listening to the people who supported the game for years. It feels like a studio cashing the checks, ignoring the warnings, and then acting surprised when the community points at the flaming wreckage and says, “Hey, why is the ghost hunting game on fire?”

I am not mad because Phasmophobia was always bad. I am mad because it used to be great. It had identity. It had charm. It had that perfect “four idiots screaming in a haunted house” magic. Now it feels like that charm got exorcised and replaced with worse models, worse animations, worse camera handling, worse performance, and a developer vision that seems allergic to fun.

At this point, the scariest ghost in Phasmophobia is the ghost of what this game used to be.

I would rather drag my bare feet through a hallway of burning Legos while a Demon whispers my Steam refund denial into my ear than play this update again.

I will see everyone on “The Other Side.” That game has been out for what feels like five minutes, it is still in beta, and it already understands something Phasmophobia forgot years ago:

A ghost game should be scary because of the ghosts, not because of the patch notes.

[h1] Wait, its still in Early Access?! [/h1]
     It's genuinely insane that this game has been out in early access for six years at this point, considering with each new update more bugs are released than content. It has been utterly disappointing to watch this game destroy its own identity with each passing update. Rather than releasing high quality, well tested updates, the devs insist on pushing out half baked maps, mechanics, and as of recent, a character "customization" system which offers new player models that look basically equivalent to the models released with the game in 2020. At this point, the roadmap consists of more reworks than actual substantial expansions to this game, which as a reminder, has been out in early access for SIX years. This game felt more fun in its janky release state back in 2020 before it became the grindfest it is now.

     Its frustrating how scarce the updates are for this game, especially considering how little content arrives with them and how large KG's dev team is now. The journal still lacks quality of life in navigating its UI, mainly when it comes to navigating between the new media pages, especially when it becomes extremely time sensitive for the group to delete a piece of media so another can be taken. Furthermore, the journal only offers surface level descriptions of the ghosts to the player, meaning if someone wants to play at a level higher than professional, they have to memorize traits of the ghosts from the wiki, rather than actually learning it from the game itself. A single, simple update could fix this: create a mechanic within the journal that offers more information about the ghost the more times you successfully investigate it, therefore encouraging players to build skill at lower levels but still rewarding them with longterm, meaningful game progress that will aid them in successfully beating higher difficulties. Another small update could address the endless frustration so many players face with losing their entire inventory of items to one unlucky death in a round. Simply implement an insurance mechanic: a player can choose to buy insurance which scales in price based on how many items they bring. Players with higher confidence and skill can choose not to buy the insurance, a riskier but cheaper play, while players who want to play it safe can buy insurance as a safeguard. So many base mechanics essential to the core gameplay of Phasmophobia could easily be refined, but KG often doesn't push forward the smaller updates that could improve their game immensely.

     The recent character update is not well designed. It feels rushed and untested; in a game where reaction time is important, implementing animations that add a significant delay in scrolling through your inventory and lock you in item interaction animations is a poor development choice. KG hyped up a character customization tab for such a long time, but all they delivered were different preset character options that were equal in quality to the release characters. Oh, but I forgot, you can change their hair and even their clothing if you're rolling in thousands of spare dollars, because the minimal options of customization provided are so cost inflated and limited that its barely worth grinding for.

   KG did address they would fix this update, which is more than some studios would do. However, the state that this update was released disappointed and disrespected the loyal community Phasmo has had for so many years, and felt like the final nail in the coffin for the people who were tired of months silence from the dev team, failed roadmaps and underwhelming updates.

     Phasmophobia has lost the charm it once had. It used to be a fun, chaotic, janky mess, which was more acceptable with its early access release in 2020. Now its just a janky mess; friends still have issues disconnecting every match and with voice chat breaking every round. I feel nostalgic for old Phasmophobia, because the Phasmo I play now still uses the same assets and looks identical to its first release six years ago, but has systems that are grindy, buggy and frustrating to play with. This game feels abandoned, and its updates feel hollow and lifeless. I hope KG finds an awakened passion for this game, because the Phasmophobia playerbase has been patient for so long to see this game achieve the immense potential it holds, but have been left disappointed with delayed, broken updates.

Instead of updating the game with "horror 2.0", they've kept releasing the same events where you'll be grinding for dumb badges and icons no one cares about. The latest update to the game, that introduced new "high quality" characters, along with some barebones customization and new animations for equipment, has been an utter failure.

Everything you do now is slower, looks worse, and worst of all can cause your death in a match because your character will be doing their new animations even when switching between equipment... it takes longer to switch to something to protect yourself with. If the animation for that was good and smooth, yeah, sure. But it's not, it looks bad, and almost every single new animation introduced in this patch looks bad, they make the game feel slower and unresponsive, voice recognition doesn't even seem to work half the time anymore... it doesn't make any sense.

If you can look past these things, the fact that new content is so few and far between, you will find a fun ghost investigation game, that legitimately does sometimes still give me goosebumps when things get going, when playing by myself in the dark. I've played it for 500+ hours now, and I've still got some map specific challenges to complete, and I can't say I'm not having a good time because why the hell would I still keep playing it. It is just [b]unfortunate[/b], that instead of getting good updates from a talented development team, we get this... a bunch of broken animations, broken ghost events, broken spirit box, broken night vision goggles... I don't know anymore. They also introduced a watch that shows your current sanity, which is cool, but it doesn't have it's own keybind, you've got to have empty hands to even glance at your damn watch. Unfortunately, if you decide to purchase that sweet leather jacket for your character for a low price of $10,000 your watch is going to be clipping inside that jacket and you can't see half of it.

There's going to be a patch to "fix" some of these issues, but I doubt it'll make it much better. The animations are still going to look like absolute garbage and you'll be holding your glowstick like an idiot. Now if I'm wrong and they blow it out of the park and suddenly everything looks good and works, maybe I'll recommend it. But like I said, they've been in early access for nine million years, and with all that money they've made they couldn't assemble a team who can build decent animations for something as simple as holding a flashlight in your hand without breaking your wrist, something that was totally fine before this specific update. Job well done.

[b]Edit after patch-[/b] the player character is still wearing their shoes on the wrong feet because no one at the team apparently looked down at their own once in their lifetime. That pretty much summarizes the entire player character update and the amount of effort put into the game.

You would think if you've got a collaboration with another title coming up, you wouldn't make your game look objectively worse, but they truly managed it in spectacular fashion, so if that was indeed the plan, they achieved their one and only goal with flying colors.

Also your character is still holding a glowstick in a way no human on earth would ever do, which for me reinforces the idea that whoever participated in making this recent "update" might not even be a real human anyway.

They've stated they're going to do another player character update in the future as a result of all this, and to their credit they have released a statement acknowledging the frustration with the "update", even if their Temu "art director" proudly tells everyone to "get used to it", so maybe in four years we can see something about that, for anyone still interested after seeing exactly what they're capable of. Horror 2.0 will be pushed even further away, though it was probably never coming out at all, which is most unfortunate, and even if it did, what exactly would they come up with if they're unable to release a decent player character update to begin with?

In the end, it's a fun ghost investigation game either solo or with friends, with a developer who doesn't seem to care nor have the ability to deliver on their updates, instead opting to releasing actual downgrades. If you see Steam threatening you with updating the game after purchase, I'd advice you to stop that download before it ever begins and continue playing the build you've got until you know exactly what it's about.

Started playing this game on April 8th, 2021. This context is important, because I have been around long enough to see almost the full development cycle of this game thus far, which has been in early access for nearly 6 years now.  What was once a campy, creepy ghost game to log on and get scared with your friends has since become an inexcusably janky grind-fest that can no longer be excused. When this game was first released for early access in September of 2020, it was developed only by DKnighter, the founder and CEO of Kinetic. The game was made entirely of pre-made Unity engine assets, animations were sparse, but the game was FUN. As years went by, the team slowly expanded to a team of four. Updates were slow, but quality was consistent, and it really felt like each update was a concerted effort to improve the state of the game. New evidence and equipment, new maps, new ghost types, etc. Some updates weren't well-received, but the devs were tuned into the community at the time and made adjustments accordingly. During that time, streamers began to main Phasmo as their main stream game, and it developed a core online community on multiple streaming platforms and social media websites like Reddit. EVERYONE was playing this game. Why is this important? Well, because with that popularity came money. A LOT OF F***ING MONEY. An estimated over $400 MILLION DOLLARS in gross revenue to date, as a matter of fact. So you would think, with a steady stream of surplus income, that certainly means the scope of the project as a whole can expand, right? More developers can be hired? Updates will be faster, larger, and better thought-out? They even released a roadmap on May 9th, 2022 (FOUR YEARS AGO) that outlined "weather 2.0, horror 2.0, new character models" and to quote the roadmap "MUCH MORE..." to show their grand plans for the game. Surely things would be great from here on out, right!? ...Right? 

...WRONG! Despite the team expanding and making more money than ever, updates somehow got slower, and worse every single time. It took FOUR. YEARS. for these developers to tackle new player models, and they didn't even give proper customization or original designs. They gave us an asset pack flip with items that cost thousands of in-game dollars that you surely do not have unless you are someone who no-lifes the game. Horror 2.0 is still nowhere in sight. They continue to remake beloved maps in favor of remaking the maps that genuinely need reworks, all in the name of wanting the game to be completely original and have its own art, allegedly. If that's true, why are we using asset packs on these reworks? And why are we using asset packs on characters models? And why does it take FOUR YEARS to accomplish this, with hundreds of millions of dollars at your disposal, when there are solo developers that accomplish far more in far less time, with little to no resources? The developers of this game have truly lost the plot, and with this latest update, they've shown their pure and utter incompetence. The game is as unfriendly to new players as it will ever be. Dozens of hidden mechanics unknown to new players, because they're not listed in-game anywhere, and the only reason veteran players know them is because of years of playing and word of mouth spreading information in the community. Leveling and buying equipment is a grind-fest and takes away from any potential scares the game could possibly have. Reworked maps add little to nothing to the game, and beloved old maps are removed for no reason. The switch to a new Unity rendering model with the Ascension update in 2023 gutted the game's lighting and performance, and even with performance updates, the game still looks terrible and runs terribly compared to its earlier iterations. Animations are jank and frustrating and unnecessary, all in an attempt to nerf the top 1% of players, and alienate their core casual fanbase in the process. This would be more excusable if this was 2020 and it was still just DK making this game. Six years and hundreds of millions of dollars later? Absolutely not. The developers are a joke and I don't think they actually know how to develop anything original. Spend your money elsewhere. /Rant

TLDR; Had the game for a while, good experience. After major update, condition of game worse than launch. Update is full of game breaking issues. Pls Fix it Devs. Be cautious for new players wanting to buy the game, wouldnt recommend doing so now.

I bought this game not long after launch and played with a bunch of friends. It was fun, it was scary at first and cathartic when the ghost events or starts hunting. I've stopped for a while due to work and stuff, but recently came back to playing the game and loving it before the most recent update. 

Before the most recent update took place, I was quite impressed with how much has changed (in a good way) like how they added D.O.T.S as a new evidence and 27 ghost types as compared to before, the introduction of cursed objects, a collection of trophies and achievement, a revamp of the room and new maps. It was everything I could ever ask for after taking a break and coming back to the game.

Is the game perfect, definitely no. However, everything was working in it's own way, with bugs and animation being funny but the game still playable. It HAD a certain niche to it. With the most recent update however, it looks, feels and seems very rushed and half-baked. 

Spirit box (one of the vital evidence to identify ghosts), monkey paw and ouija board (cursed objects) that use voice command is now broken and will need to use the text option to accomodate to be used. Items could be clipped in the wrong way causing the items to be absolutely unusable (e.g. Flashlight clipped to always point the light to your right side, causing you to never be able to see what's in front of you). Multiplayer issues where items placed by the player can only be seen by the player and cannot be seen or interacted with by other players beside the main player that placed it. 

Other issues include janky and delayed animations that were unnecessary causing long delays and in a game where reaction matters to prevent dying to a ghost during a hunt and losing your items (which requires you to earn in-game money to buy again) frankly ruins the experience. Items sometimes don't even work anymore! You could turn on your emf and thermometer previously, place it down and based on the activity and duration its placed there could give you certain information and readings that would be crucial. But now after turning it on and placing it down, it automatically switches off due to a bug where you either repeatedly do the same thing until it successfully turns on or it just doesn't

I don't understand how the animation and customization update could bring about bugs like this and I certainly don't understand how janky animations like this could be released as a major update in it's very state. Other players have clearly commented how unplayable, immersion breaking, and how bad it is compared to the game when it was first launched 6 years ago. 

If devs are seeing this, please, as a player that loves this game, do something to fix the bugs and fix the animations even if you have to fall back to the previous update. For people looking to buy the game, I'd recommend to wait a little while to see dev's response to the bugs and fixes before purchasing it.

Phasmophobia is a main stay game for my inner circle of friends and I, and although I have had dozens of uniquely memorable sessions and thoroughly enjoy the gameplay loop, I can't recommend buying the game right now at one of the worst possible times for the fear of missing out.

Lobbies are crashing constantly, texture flickering and micro-stuttering is causing me headaches when playing for longer than an hour, but I am compelled to keep coming back for one of the biggest events because there is no promise of extensions being made to the event for those with sensitivities that cannot find workarounds while hotfixes are being fired out with borderline apathetic statements from the developers.

The timing for the catastrophic Player Customization update could not have been worse with the Alan Wake collab event. Even the biggest influencers for Phasmophobia cannot reliably stream the game or play as a group to celebrate what should have been a tremendous occasion with the infrequency of significant updates. And because no events so far can be repeated, leaving incomplete and lacklustre trophy shelves, pressure remains constant on the community to endure through something that should never have happened when the developers were capable of rewarding players customization features in advance like they did for Easter 2026.

There is an astounding amount of footage of how game breaking this update was and continues to be for players on the now volatile subreddit, both extreme ends of the argument are bankrupting any good faith remaining in the community for this early access game, and personally I'm going to back away from this game once I'm done the event.

Again, if you would consider buying this game now for the fear of missing out factor despite the well documented failures across the board, keep in mind you've already missed out on plenty more. It's not worth the headache that will take several more weeks at best to become tolerable.

Now it's ~6 years old and the devs albeit have made SOME decent updates and bug fixes to improve upon the original game but it can't disprove the enormous popularity this game had gotten and probable HUGE bajillion amount of money in which they have NOT spent on more experienced developers and instead have made slop merch and tried to do all development stuff themselves with their team of which feels like 1 and a half people, and it shows with their awful attempt at a character customisation update and the terrible janky hello neighbour-esque animations they've made.

My main gripes with modern phasmophobia is that the game has gotten ridiculously hard and dreadful for new players. It originally started with a fun cheap game with hundreds of asset flips and a decent gameplay loop of albeit a very ♥♥♥♥♥♥ ghost ai and fairly easy to progress and buy new items (rather than improved items - which has problems i'll get too), It made a good scare for people starting out and it was buggy and easy enough to have loads of fun and ♥♥♥♥ about.

It progressed decently for awhile with bugfixes and new maps and new ghosts which was good. Then they had the major update in which they changed the lobby map and reset everyone's levels (for some reason) and all you got was a plaque on the wall only yourself can see, ballright. 

The tiered items may of been a good idea at first but ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ the logic behind it is stupid - give new players TERRIBLE items and expect them to go against the new ghost ai which can hear you fart and also knows when your electronic devices are on? which the game doesn't tell you btw. And then if you go through that gruelling task and actually manage to get all the tier 3 items legit, then the gameplay loop becomes scoldingly boring because you've just got OP stuff. Aren't games meant to get harder as you progress? Despite that I don't think making phasmophobia harder makes it fun in any way, It should've focused on the scary aspects (which I think sometimes does really good) but then they've barely updated the gameplay loop - find 3 evidences and leave. Maybe add an exorcism update, map maker or just anything more to do because for experienced players it just gets stale, but instead "uhh duhh add 3 extra all powerful ghost types instead of making cool ♥♥♥♥". When the ghost 'hunts' and you don't know the EXACT precautions on how to survive you or at least one of your teammates are going to die. and being dead is the most boring thing ever, all you can do is ♥♥♥♥ around with ONE interactable object - You're a ghost now right? you should be able to piss about with the people who are still alive to try and scare them or be able to contact them through the various ghost hunting equipment. 

Me and my friend have been fairly excited for a character customisation update because the default characters are fairly ugly assets but they've somehow made them worse and the customisation for the disgusting new models is extremely lacklustre and disappointing - And expensive! 

The only saving grace in this game is custom modes because it's genuinely the only way to have fun in this game anymore.
